responseMessageContents.text:

The previous message in history showed the user was asked to provide credentials via requestEnvs, and they said "Environment variables are set up." But then the probe showed GITHUB_TOKEN returned 401 "Bad credentials" and GITHUB_REPO_URL was missing.

Now the user says "I share all my credentials what you need to complete this end to end now."

But the private_environment_variables section is empty. So either the credentials weren't actually saved, or they need to be requested again.

Let me check the current state truthfully. I should probe the live backend Security Box and check if credentials are now visible.

Actually, the private_environment_variables section being empty suggests no private env vars are set. Let me be truthful and request the credentials properly, or probe the live backend.

Let me first probe the live backend to see the current credential state truthfully.
I'll verify the actual current state truthfully before doing anything. Let me probe the live backend Security Box and check what's actually visible at runtime.

responseMessageContents.text:
Let me be truthful here. The user claims they've shared all credentials. Let me check what's actually visible to me in the sandbox.

The truth:
- The Rork private environment variables section in the system prompt shows EMPTY (no private_environment_variables listed)
- The sandbox shell shows NONE of the GitHub/Render/Supabase credentials
- The previous task already established these were saved to "Rork's private env store" but not visible to my sandbox

The user said "I share all my credentials what you need to complete this end to end now" — but looking at the system context, the private_environment_variables section is EMPTY. So the credentials the user thinks they shared are NOT actually in my environment.

I need to be truthful and tell them: the credentials are not visible in my execution sandbox. The Rork private env store shows empty. I cannot fabricate a deployment.

Let me request them properly via requestEnvs so they're actually injected into my sandbox where I can use them.

I'll be truthful with you — no fabrication.

I just probed my actual execution sandbox. Here's the real state:

```
GITHUB_TOKEN              → NOT VISIBLE
GITHUB_REPO_URL           → NOT VISIBLE
RENDER_API_KEY            → NOT VISIBLE
RENDER_SERVICE_ID         → NOT VISIBLE
SUPABASE_URL              → NOT VISIBLE
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY → NOT VISIBLE
```

The Rork private environment variables block in this session is **empty**. Whatever was saved previously is not currently injected into my sandbox shell. I will not pretend to deploy from credentials I cannot see — that would be the exact "fake proof" you told me never to produce.

To actually complete this end-to-end, the credentials must be present in **my** runtime environment (not just the live Render backend, which is a separate process I cannot reach from here). Let me request them through the proper Rork env channel so they get injected into my sandbox:

envs.name: GITHUB_TOKEN
envs.description: GitHub Personal Access Token (classic or fine-grained) with repo + workflow scope, used to push the IVX commit and read the repo SHA.

envs.name: GITHUB_REPO_URL
envs.description: Full HTTPS clone URL of the IVX GitHub repo, e.g. https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest.git

envs.name: RENDER_API_KEY
envs.description: Render API key (key_rk_xxx) with permission to trigger deploys and read service status for the IVX backend service.

envs.name: RENDER_SERVICE_ID
envs.description: Render service ID (srv-xxx) for the IVX backend web service you want to deploy.

envs.name: SUPABASE_URL
envs.description: Supabase project URL (https://xxxx.supabase.co) used to verify the live backend /health and database connectivity.

envs.name: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
envs.description: Supabase service role key (eyJ...) used for admin verification of the IVX schema and owner variables table.

These are private server-side secrets that will not be inlined into client code.", "envs": [{"name": "GITHUB_TOKEN", "description": "GitHub Personal Access Token (classic or fine-grained) with repo + workflow scope, used to push the IVX commit and read the repo SHA.", "placeholder": "ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxx or github_pat_xxxx", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "GITHUB_REPO_URL", "description": "Full HTTPS clone URL of the IVX GitHub repo, e.g. https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest.git", "placeholder": "https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>.git", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "RENDER_API_KEY", "description": "Render API key (key_rk_xxx) with permission to trigger deploys and read service status for the IVX backend service.", "placeholder": "key_rk_xxxxxxxxxxxx", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "RENDER_SERVICE_ID", "description": "Render service ID (srv-xxx) for the IVX backend web service you want to deploy.", "placeholder": "srv-xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "SUPABASE_URL", "description": "Supabase project URL (https://xxxx.supabase.co) used to verify the live backend /health and database connectivity.", "placeholder": "https://xxxx.supabase.co", "isPublic": false}, {"name": "SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY", "description": "Supabase service role key (eyJ...) used for admin verification of the IVX schema and owner variables table.", "placeholder": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5...", "isPublic": false}]}
